title: Richly Fermented Dutch Plant-Based Rerremen
description: A hearty, fermented, plant-based dish from the Netherlands featuring crispy chilli flakes and crunchy deep-fried tofu, all wrapped up in a tender agave leaf.

ingredients.name: Tofu
ingredients.notes: firm, drained, cut into small cubes

ingredients.name: Fermentation mix
ingredients.notes: combine equal parts brown rice flour, seaweed powder, and apple cider vinegar

instructions: Wrap each agave leaf in a sheet of parchment paper for easy cleanup.
Deep-fry the tofu cubes in hot oil until crispy and golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the tofu from the oil and let it cool on a paper towel-lined plate.
In a blender or food processor, combine the fermentation mix, chilli flakes, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and water. Blend until smooth.
Season the fermentation mix with salt to taste.
Place a piece of agave leaf on a flat surface and spoon a quarter of the fermentation mix onto one half of the leaf.
Top the fermentation mix with a few pieces of the deep-fried tofu.
Fold the agave leaf in half to enclose the filling, and secure with a toothpick if needed.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make 3 more Rerremen.
Place the Rerremen on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the agave leaves are tender and the filling is heated through.
Serve the Rerremen hot, garnished with additional chilli flakes if desired.
